findings.title: Overview establishes mission, domain context, and design rationale concisely
findings.detail: The 7-line Overview covers what the agent does (feedback-to-parameter translation), domain constraints (agent cannot hear songs, users range from musicians to casual listeners), five feedback types, and design rationale (why triage-first matters). This is exactly the kind of context that enables intelligent improvisation when situations don't match the script.

findings.title: A&R collaborator persona is load-bearing and consistent throughout
findings.detail: The 'music producer's A&R collaborator' framing carries through all workflow steps — celebrating positive feedback, using natural conversational prompts, bridging emotional vocabulary to technical parameters. This persona context directly shapes output quality for a companion-interactive skill.

findings.title: Good use of mandatory reference loading for detailed content
findings.detail: Step 3 directs 'Load references/feedback-triage-guide.md for detailed classification rules' and Step 4a/4e direct loading of suno-parameter-map.md. These are mandatory directives, not suggestive. The triage guide and parameter map contain substantial domain knowledge that would bloat SKILL.md if inlined.

findings.title: SKILL.md at 6303 tokens exceeds single-purpose agent guideline of ~5000 tokens
findings.detail: While the 336-line count is within the 500-line allowance for single-purpose agents with deep persona, the token estimate of 6303 exceeds the ~5000 token guideline by ~26%. The inline output template (lines 269-301) and headless JSON contract (lines 28-47) account for roughly 800-1000 tokens that could be extracted.
findings.action: Extract the Step 6 output format template (lines 269-301) to references/output-template.md and the headless output contract JSON (lines 28-47) to references/headless-contract.md. Replace with brief descriptions and mandatory load directives. This would bring SKILL.md closer to ~5000 tokens.

findings.title: Step 4e Technical Resolution contains dense domain knowledge that could be in a reference
findings.detail: Lines 184-205 detail specific resolution paths for artifacts, vocal quality, timing, pronunciation, quality degradation, instrument bleed, and section-specific issues including tier-specific Studio feature guidance. This is ~22 lines of Suno-specific technical knowledge that overlaps with what suno-parameter-map.md likely covers. The density is high but each item is distinct and actionable.
findings.action: Consider whether the Studio feature resolution paths (Legacy Editor, Replace Section, Stems workflow) are already covered in suno-parameter-map.md. If so, replace the inline detail with a mandatory load directive. If not, this content is appropriately placed as workflow-critical instructions.

findings.title: Step 7 references iteration log acceptance 'in Step 2' but Step 2 does not mention it
findings.detail: Line 329 says 'Accept iteration log on re-invocation: If the user provides a previous iteration log in Step 2...' but Step 2 (Gather Context, lines 83-100) does not mention accepting or handling iteration logs from previous sessions. This creates a gap where the agent might not recognize iteration logs during context gathering.
findings.action: Add a bullet to Step 2's 'Valuable context' list: 'Previous iteration log — Did the user provide a log from a prior feedback session? If yes, load it to track what was tried and what worked.'

findings.title: On Activation includes config fallback for resilience
findings.detail: Line 57 specifies 'If bmad-init is unavailable, greet generically and default communication_language to English. Do not block the workflow.' This graceful degradation prevents the skill from stalling when dependencies are unavailable.

findings.title: Good script/LLM boundary in adjustment mapping workflow
findings.detail: Step 5 correctly delegates structured dimension extraction and parameter mapping to scripts (parse-feedback.py, map-adjustments.py) while reserving LLM judgment for refining recommendations based on full context, band profile, and creative intent. This is the right boundary — scripts handle deterministic lookups, the LLM handles semantic interpretation.

findings.title: Vague feedback elicitation includes non-convergence fallbacks
findings.detail: Steps 4c and 4d both include fallback strategies when elicitation doesn't converge — suggesting 2-3 variants to turn an elicitation problem into a selection problem. This shows mature outcome-driven design that handles real-world failure modes rather than assuming the happy path.

findings.title: Research mandate in Step 5 adds web search dependency
findings.detail: Line 221 directs the agent to use web search to verify Suno-specific recommendations against current behavior, with graceful fallback to training knowledge. This is appropriate given Suno's rapid model evolution but adds a tool dependency. The fallback clause makes this resilient.

skillmd_assessment.notes: SKILL.md is well-structured for a companion-interactive agent with strong domain framing and consistent persona voice. At 6303 tokens it's modestly over the ~5000 guideline — the output template and headless contract are the main extraction candidates. Progressive disclosure to references is already well-implemented for triage rules and parameter maps.

summary.assessment: Well-crafted companion-interactive skill with strong persona context, good progressive disclosure, and correct intelligence placement boundaries. The main improvement opportunity is extracting inline templates to references to reduce SKILL.md token count from ~6300 to ~5000.
summary.top_improvement: Extract the Step 6 output format template and headless JSON contract to reference files to bring SKILL.md within the ~5000 token guideline for single-purpose agents.
